info.title: Nuchi API
info.description: OpenAPI source for the Go backend replacement. This contract documents the current Hono resource behavior from docs/specs/18-go-backend-replacement/api-parity-fixtures.md, while intentionally changing auth to owned email/password sessions, Bearer access tokens, HttpOnly refresh-token cookies, structured API errors, and required transaction currency with ARS as the default.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Owned auth endpoints. Auth responses are not wrapped in the app resource `{ data: ... }` envelope.

post.description: Intentional migration behavior: creates an owned auth user and starts email verification. Current Hono uses Clerk and has no equivalent resource endpoint. The response is an auth shape, not an app resource envelope.

201.description: User registered and verification email queued.

post.summary: Log in with email and password
post.description: Intentional migration behavior: returns a short-lived Bearer access token and sets the refresh token in an HttpOnly cookie. Current Hono uses Clerk and resource handlers read Clerk auth. The response is not wrapped in `{ data: ... }`.
Intentional migration behavior: uses the HttpOnly refresh-token cookie to issue a new Bearer access token. The refresh cookie may be rotated.

200.description: A new access token was issued.

post.description: Intentional migration behavior: revokes the refresh token identified by the refresh-token cookie and clears that cookie.
Intentional migration behavior: consumes a one-time email verification token.
Intentional migration behavior: queues a reset email when the account is eligible. The response does not disclose whether the email exists.
Intentional migration behavior: consumes a valid reset token and replaces the password.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: lists accounts owned by the authenticated user and returns `{ data: [{ id, name }] }`.

post.description: Current Hono behavior: creates an owned account and returns the full account row in `{ data }`. Duplicate names are case-insensitive per user and return 409.
Current Hono behavior: deletes owned accounts matching the requested IDs and ignores missing or unowned IDs. Account deletion cascades transactions through the database foreign key.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: returns one owned account as `{ data: { id, name } }` or 404 when missing or unowned.

patch.description: Current Hono behavior: updates an owned account and returns the full account row in `{ data }`. Duplicate names are case-insensitive per user and return 409.

delete.description: Current Hono behavior: deletes one owned account and returns `{ data: { id } }`. Account deletion cascades transactions through the database foreign key.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: lists categories owned by the authenticated user and returns `{ data: [{ id, name }] }`.

post.description: Current Hono behavior: creates an owned category and returns the full category row in `{ data }`. Duplicate names are case-insensitive per user and return 409.

Current Hono behavior: deletes owned categories matching the requested IDs and ignores missing or unowned IDs. Category deletion clears matching transaction `categoryId` values through the database foreign key.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: returns one owned category as `{ data: { id, name } }` or 404 when missing or unowned.

patch.description: Current Hono behavior: updates an owned category and returns the full category row in `{ data }`. Intentional contract decision: duplicate category updates return the same structured 409 as duplicate creates instead of the current Hono 500 mismatch.

delete.description: Current Hono behavior: deletes one owned category and returns `{ data: { id } }`. Category deletion clears matching transaction `categoryId` values through the database foreign key.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: lists transactions joined through owned accounts, supports `from`, `to`, and `accountId`, defaults to the last 30 days, uses inclusive date filtering, rejects ranges over 366 days, and sorts by date descending. Intentional migration behavior: each transaction includes required `currency`, defaulting to `ARS`.

post.description: Current Hono behavior: validates owned account and optional owned category before inserting and returns the full transaction row in `{ data }`. Intentional migration behavior: `currency` is required in the contract and defaults to `ARS`.

Current Hono behavior: accepts 1 to 500 transactions as a JSON array, rejects numeric `Content-Length` over 1,000,000 bytes, validates all rows and owned account/category references before inserting, and returns all inserted rows in `{ data }`. Intentional migration behavior: each row includes required `currency`, defaulting to `ARS`.

Current Hono behavior: accepts 1 to 500 IDs, rejects numeric `Content-Length` over 100,000 bytes, deletes owned transactions matching requested IDs, ignores missing or unowned IDs, and returns deleted IDs in `{ data }`.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: returns one transaction joined through an owned account or 404 when missing or unowned. Intentional migration behavior: the returned transaction includes required `currency`, defaulting to `ARS`.

patch.description: Current Hono behavior: validates the target transaction, owned account, and optional owned category before updating and returns the full transaction row in `{ data }`. Intentional migration behavior: `currency` is required in the contract and defaults to `ARS`.

delete.description: Current Hono behavior: deletes one owned transaction and returns `{ data: { id } }`.

get.description: Current Hono behavior: returns dashboard totals, change percentages, expense category breakdown, and daily income/expense series for the selected inclusive date range. The endpoint supports the same `from`, `to`, and `accountId` filters as transaction list, defaults to the last 30 days, and rejects ranges over 366 days. Amounts remain integer milliunits and use the single-currency ARS default until multi-currency UX exists.

bearerAuth.description: Short-lived access token sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. App resource endpoints require this scheme unless documented otherwise.

refreshTokenCookie.name: nuchi_refresh_token
refreshTokenCookie.description: HttpOnly refresh-token cookie set by login/refresh and consumed by refresh/logout. JavaScript clients must not read this value directly.

FromDate.name: from
FromDate.description: Inclusive start date. Must use `yyyy-MM-dd`; parsed as start of day. Defaults to 30 days before current server time.

ToDate.name: to
ToDate.description: Inclusive end date. Must use `yyyy-MM-dd`; parsed as end of day. Defaults to current server time.

AccountIdQuery.name: accountId
AccountIdQuery.description: Optional account filter. Missing or unowned accounts produce an empty list/summary because resource queries still require an owned joined account.

RefreshTokenSetCookie.description: Sets or rotates the HttpOnly refresh-token cookie. The cookie is scoped to auth refresh/logout paths where practical.

RefreshTokenClearCookie.description: Clears the HttpOnly refresh-token cookie after logout.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying a rate-limited request.

ValidationError.description: The request body, route parameter, or query string is invalid. Intentional migration behavior: Go handlers return this structured shape instead of Hono/Zod validator's package-default validation body.

InvalidDateQueryError.description: The date range query is invalid.

error.message: from and to must use yyyy-MM-dd dates.
from must be less than or equal to to.

UnauthorizedError.description: Authentication failed or the Bearer access token is missing. Current Hono resource handlers return plain `{ "error": "Unauthorized" }`; the Go contract intentionally uses the structured shared error shape.

InvalidRefreshTokenError.description: The refresh-token cookie is missing, expired, revoked, or invalid.

error.message: Refresh token is invalid or expired.

InvalidTokenError.description: The submitted one-time token is missing, expired, already used, or invalid.

EmailNotVerifiedError.description: The login credentials are valid, but the email address must be verified first.

error.message: Email address must be verified before login.

ConflictError.description: The request conflicts with an existing resource or account state.

DuplicateAccountNameError.description: The authenticated user already has an account with this name. Names are case-insensitive per user.

error.message: You already have an account with this name.

DuplicateCategoryNameError.description: The authenticated user already has a category with this name. Names are case-insensitive per user.

error.message: You already have a category with this name.

AccountNotFoundError.description: The account is missing or is not owned by the authenticated user.

CategoryNotFoundError.description: The category is missing or is not owned by the authenticated user.

TransactionNotFoundError.description: The transaction is missing or is not reachable through an account owned by the authenticated user.

TransactionReferenceNotFoundError.description: The target transaction, account reference, or category reference is missing or unowned.

RequestBodyTooLargeError.description: The request body exceeds a documented bulk operation size limit.

TransactionMutationRateLimitError.description: The transaction mutation exceeded the per-user/action rate limit: 60 requests per 60 seconds.

error.message: Too many transaction mutations. Please try again later.

DatabaseError.description: The API could not complete the request because of a database error.

RateLimitError.description: The request exceeded a documented rate limit.

error.message: Too many requests. Please try again later.

message.description: Human-readable error summary safe to show in the app.

details.description: Optional structured context for validation fields, database constraints, limits, or other endpoint-specific details.

AuthMessageResponse.description: Auth command success shape. Auth endpoints intentionally do not use the app resource `{ data: ... }` envelope.

AuthSessionResponse.description: Auth session success shape returned by login and refresh. The refresh token is delivered by HttpOnly cookie, not JSON.

accessToken.description: JWT access token for Bearer API requests.

ResourceId.description: Opaque resource ID. Legacy resources use cuid-style text IDs; auth users use UUIDs.

CurrencyCode.description: Transaction currency. The Go replacement starts with ARS as the required/default currency until multi-currency UX exists.

userId.description: Authenticated user owner ID. Current Hono rows use Clerk IDs; the Go replacement uses internal UUIDs.
Authenticated user owner ID. Current Hono rows use Clerk IDs; the Go replacement uses internal UUIDs.

amount.description: Signed integer milliunits. Positive values are income; negative values are expenses.

income.description: Income sum in milliunits for this day.

expenses.description: Absolute expense sum in milliunits for this day.

remainingAmount.description: Sum of signed transaction amounts in milliunits.

remainingChange.description: Percentage change versus the previous period.

incomeAmount.description: Sum of non-negative transaction amounts in milliunits.

incomeChange.description: Percentage change versus the previous period.

expensesAmount.description: Sum of absolute negative transaction amounts in milliunits.

expensesChange.description: Percentage change versus the previous period.

categories.description: Top three negative categorized expense groups, plus an `Other` group when more categories remain.

days.description: One entry for every calendar day in the selected range, filling missing income/expense with 0.
